children.heading: Commencement date for payment of benefits
children.label: a Commencement date for payment of benefits

children.text: occurs the date on which the participant attains the earlier of age 65 or the normal retirement age specified under the plan,
occurs the 10th anniversary of the year in which the participant commenced participation in the plan, or
the participant terminates his service with the employer.

children.heading: Decrease in plan benefits by reason of increases in benefit levels under Social Security Act or Railroad Retirement Act of 1937
children.label: b Decrease in plan benefits by reason of increases in benefit levels under Social Security Act or Railroad Retirement Act of 1937

children.text: a participant or beneficiary is receiving benefits under a pension plan, or
a participant is separated from the service and has non-forfeitable rights to benefits,

children.heading: Forfeiture of accrued benefits derived from employer contributions
children.label: c Forfeiture of accrued benefits derived from employer contributions
children.text: No pension plan may provide that any part of a participant’s accrued benefit derived from employer contributions (whether or not otherwise nonforfeitable) is forfeitable solely because of withdrawal by such participant of any amount attributable to the benefit derived from contributions made by such participant. The preceding sentence shall not apply (1) to the accrued benefit of any participant unless, at the time of such withdrawal, such participant has a nonforfeitable right to at least 50 percent of such accrued benefit, or (2) to the extent that an accrued benefit is permitted to be forfeited in accordance with .section 1053(a)(3)(D)(iii) of this title

children.heading: Assignment or alienation of plan benefits
children.label: d Assignment or alienation of plan benefits

children.text: Each pension plan shall provide that benefits provided under the plan may not be assigned or alienated.

For the purposes of paragraph (1) of this subsection, there shall not be taken into account any voluntary and revocable assignment of not to exceed 10 percent of any benefit payment, or of any irrevocable assignment or alienation of benefits executed before . The preceding sentence shall not apply to any assignment or alienation made for the purposes of defraying plan administration costs. For purposes of this paragraph a loan made to a participant or beneficiary shall not be treated as an assignment or alienation if such loan is secured by the participant’s accrued non-forfeitable benefit and is exempt from the tax imposed by (relating to tax on prohibited transactions) by reason of .September 2, 1974section 4975 of title 26section 4975(d)(1) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall apply to the creation, assignment, or recognition of a right to any benefit payable with respect to a participant pursuant to a domestic relations order, except that paragraph (1) shall not apply if the order is determined to be a qualified domestic relations order. Each pension plan shall provide for the payment of benefits in accordance with the applicable requirements of any qualified domestic relations order.

children.label: I
children.text: which creates or recognizes the existence of an alternate payee’s right to, or assigns to an alternate payee the right to, receive all or a portion of the benefits payable with respect to a participant under a plan, and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to which the requirements of subparagraphs (C) and (D) are met, and

children.label: I
children.text: relates to the provision of child support, alimony payments, or marital property rights to a spouse, former spouse, child, or other dependent of a participant, and

children.label: II
children.text: is made pursuant to a State or Tribal domestic relations law (including a community property law).

children.label: i
children.text: the name and the last known mailing address (if any) of the participant and the name and mailing address of each alternate payee covered by the order,

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount or percentage of the participant’s benefits to be paid by the plan to each such alternate payee, or the manner in which such amount or percentage is to be determined,

children.label: iii
children.text: the number of payments or period to which such order applies, and

children.label: iv
children.text: each plan to which such order applies.

children.label: i
children.text: does not require a plan to provide any type or form of benefit, or any option, not otherwise provided under the plan,

children.label: ii
children.text: does not require the plan to provide increased benefits (determined on the basis of actuarial value), and

children.label: iii
children.text: does not require the payment of benefits to an alternate payee which are required to be paid to another alternate payee under another order previously determined to be a qualified domestic relations order.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of any payment before a participant has separated from service, on or after the date on which the participant attains (or would have attained) the earliest retirement age,

children.label: II
children.text: as if the participant had retired on the date on which such payment is to begin under such order (but taking into account only the present value of benefits actually accrued and not taking into account the present value of any employer subsidy for early retirement), and

children.label: III
children.text: in any form in which such benefits may be paid under the plan to the participant (other than in the form of a joint and survivor annuity with respect to the alternate payee and his or her subsequent spouse).

children.label: I
children.text: the date on which the participant is entitled to a distribution under the plan, or

children.label: II
children.text: the later of the date of the participant attains age 50 or the earliest date on which the participant could begin receiving benefits under the plan if the participant separated from service.1So in original. The word “of” probably should not appear.1

children.label: i
children.text: the former spouse of a participant shall be treated as a surviving spouse of such participant for purposes of (and any spouse of the participant shall not be treated as a spouse of the participant for such purposes), andsection 1055 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: if married for at least 1 year, the surviving former spouse shall be treated as meeting the requirements of .section 1055(f) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the plan administrator shall promptly notify the participant and each alternate payee of the receipt of such order and the plan’s procedures for determining the qualified status of domestic relations orders, and

children.label: II
children.text: within a reasonable period after receipt of such order, the plan administrator shall determine whether such order is a qualified domestic relations order and notify the participant and each alternate payee of such determination.

children.label: I
children.text: shall be in writing,

children.label: II
children.text: shall provide for the notification of each person specified in a domestic relations order as entitled to payment of benefits under the plan (at the address included in the domestic relations order) of such procedures promptly upon receipt by the plan of the domestic relations order, and

children.label: III
children.text: shall permit an alternate payee to designate a representative for receipt of copies of notices that are sent to the alternate payee with respect to a domestic relations order.

children.label: i
children.text: During any period in which the issue of whether a domestic relations order is a qualified domestic relations order is being determined (by the plan administrator, by a court of competent jurisdiction, or otherwise), the plan administrator shall separately account for the amounts (hereinafter in this subparagraph referred to as the “segregated amounts”) which would have been payable to the alternate payee during such period if the order had been determined to be a qualified domestic relations order.

children.label: ii
children.text: If within the 18-month period described in clause (v) the order (or modification thereof) is determined to be a qualified domestic relations order, the plan administrator shall pay the segregated amounts (including any interest thereon) to the person or persons entitled thereto.

children.label: I
children.text: it is determined that the order is not a qualified domestic relations order, or

children.label: II
children.text: the issue as to whether such order is a qualified domestic relations order is not resolved,

children.label: iv
children.text: Any determination that an order is a qualified domestic relations order which is made after the close of the 18-month period described in clause (v) shall be applied prospectively only.

children.label: v
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the 18-month period described in this clause is the 18-month period beginning with the date on which the first payment would be required to be made under the domestic relations order.

children.label: i
children.text: treating a domestic relations order as being (or not being) a qualified domestic relations order, or

children.label: ii
children.text: taking action under subparagraph (H),

children.label: J
children.text: A person who is an alternate payee under a qualified domestic relations order shall be considered for purposes of any provision of this chapter a beneficiary under the plan. Nothing in the preceding sentence shall permit a requirement under of the payment of more than 1 premium with respect to a participant for any period.section 1301 of this title

children.label: K
children.text: The term “alternate payee” means any spouse, former spouse, child, or other dependent of a participant who is recognized by a domestic relations order as having a right to receive all, or a portion of, the benefits payable under a plan with respect to such participant.

children.label: L
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any plan to which paragraph (1) does not apply.

children.label: M
children.text: Payment of benefits by a pension plan in accordance with the applicable requirements of a qualified domestic relations order shall not be treated as garnishment for purposes of .section 1673(a) of title 15

children.label: N
children.text: In prescribing regulations under this paragraph, the Secretary shall consult with the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: i
children.text: under a judgment of conviction for a crime involving such plan,

children.label: ii
children.text: under a civil judgment (including a consent order or decree) entered by a court in an action brought in connection with a violation (or alleged violation) of part 4 of this subtitle, or

children.label: iii
children.text: pursuant to a settlement agreement between the Secretary and the participant, or a settlement agreement between the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation and the participant, in connection with a violation (or alleged violation) of part 4 of this subtitle by a fiduciary or any other person,

children.label: B
children.text: the judgment, order, decree, or settlement agreement expressly provides for the offset of all or part of the amount ordered or required to be paid to the plan against the participant’s benefits provided under the plan, and

children.label: I
children.text: such spouse has consented in writing to such offset and such consent is witnessed by a notary public or representative of the plan (or it is established to the satisfaction of a plan representative that such consent may not be obtained by reason of circumstances described in ), orsection 1055(c)(2)(B) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: an election to waive the right of the spouse to a qualified joint and survivor annuity or a qualified preretirement survivor annuity is in effect in accordance with the requirements of ,section 1055(c) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: such spouse is ordered or required in such judgment, order, decree, or settlement to pay an amount to the plan in connection with a violation of part 4 of this subtitle, or

children.label: iii
children.text: in such judgment, order, decree, or settlement, such spouse retains the right to receive the survivor annuity under a qualified joint and survivor annuity provided pursuant to and under a qualified preretirement survivor annuity provided pursuant to , determined in accordance with paragraph (5).section 1055(a)(1) of this titlesection 1055(a)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the participant terminated employment on the date of the offset,

children.label: ii
children.text: there was no offset,

children.label: iii
children.text: the plan permitted commencement of benefits only on or after normal retirement age,

children.label: iv
children.text: the plan provided only the minimum-required qualified joint and survivor annuity, and

children.label: v
children.text: the amount of the qualified preretirement survivor annuity under the plan is equal to the amount of the survivor annuity payable under the minimum-required qualified joint and survivor annuity.

children.label: B
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “minimum-required qualified joint and survivor annuity” means the qualified joint and survivor annuity which is the actuarial equivalent of the participant’s accrued benefit (within the meaning of ) and under which the survivor annuity is 50 percent of the amount of the annuity which is payable during the joint lives of the participant and the spouse.section 1002(23) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on distributions other than life annuities paid by plan
children.label: e Limitation on distributions other than life annuities paid by plan

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this part, the fiduciary of a pension plan that is subject to the additional funding requirements of shall not permit a prohibited payment to be made from a plan during a period in which such plan has a liquidity shortfall (as defined in ).section 1083(j)(4) of this titlesection 1083(j)(4)(E)(i) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: any payment, in excess of the monthly amount paid under a single life annuity (plus any social security supplements described in the last sentence of ), to a participant or beneficiary whose annuity starting date (as defined in ), that occurs during the period referred to in paragraph (1),section 1054(b)(1)(G) of this titlesection 1055(h)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: any payment for the purchase of an irrevocable commitment from an insurer to pay benefits, and

children.label: C
children.text: any other payment specified by the Secretary of the Treasury by regulations.

children.heading: Period of shortfall
children.label: 3 Period of shortfall
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a plan has a liquidity shortfall during the period that there is an underpayment of an installment under by reason of .section 1083(j)(3) of this titlesection 1083(j)(4)(A) of this title

children.heading: Coordination with other provisions
children.label: 4 Coordination with other provisions
children.text: Compliance with this subsection shall not constitute a violation of any other provision of this chapter.

children.heading: Missing participants in terminated plans
children.label: f Missing participants in terminated plans
children.text: In the case of a plan covered by , upon termination of the plan, benefits of missing participants shall be treated in accordance with .section 1350 of this titlesection 1350 of this title

children.heading: Funding-based limits on benefits and benefit accruals under single-employer plans
children.label: g Funding-based limits on benefits and benefit accruals under single-employer plans

children.heading: Funding-based limitation on shutdown benefits and other unpredictable contingent event benefits under single-employer plans
children.label: 1 Funding-based limitation on shutdown benefits and other unpredictable contingent event benefits under single-employer plans
i
children.text: is less than 60 percent, or

children.label: ii
children.text: would be less than 60 percent taking into account such occurrence.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of subparagraph (A)(i), the amount of the increase in the funding target of the plan (under ) for the plan year attributable to the occurrence referred to in subparagraph (A), andsection 1083 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of subparagraph (A)(ii), the amount sufficient to result in an adjusted funding target attainment percentage of 60 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: a plant shutdown (or similar event, as determined by the Secretary of the Treasury), or

children.label: ii
children.text: an event other than the attainment of any age, performance of any service, receipt or derivation of any compensation, or occurrence of death or disability.

children.heading: Limitations on plan amendments increasing liability for benefits
children.label: 2 Limitations on plan amendments increasing liability for benefits
i
children.text: less than 80 percent, or

children.label: ii
children.text: would be less than 80 percent taking into account such amendment.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of subparagraph (A)(i), the amount of the increase in the funding target of the plan (under ) for the plan year attributable to the amendment, andsection 1083 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of subparagraph (A)(ii), the amount sufficient to result in an adjusted funding target attainment percentage of 80 percent.

children.heading: Exception for certain benefit increases
children.label: C Exception for certain benefit increases
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any amendment which provides for an increase in benefits under a formula which is not based on a participant’s compensation, but only if the rate of such increase is not in excess of the contemporaneous rate of increase in average wages of participants covered by the amendment.

children.heading: Funding percentage less than 60 percent
children.label: A Funding percentage less than 60 percent
children.text: A defined benefit plan which is a single-employer plan shall provide that, in any case in which the plan’s adjusted funding target attainment percentage for a plan year is less than 60 percent, the plan may not pay any prohibited payment after the valuation date for the plan year.

children.heading: Bankruptcy
children.label: B Bankruptcy
children.text: A defined benefit plan which is a single-employer plan shall provide that, during any period in which the plan sponsor is a debtor in a case under title 11 or similar Federal or State law, the plan may not pay any prohibited payment. The preceding sentence shall not apply on or after the date on which the enrolled actuary of the plan certifies that the adjusted funding target attainment percentage of such plan (determined by not taking into account any adjustment of segment rates under ) is not less than 100 percent.section 1083(h)(2)(C)(iv) of this title

children.heading: Limited payment if percentage at least 60 percent but less than 80 percent
children.label: C Limited payment if percentage at least 60 percent but less than 80 percent
I
children.text: 50 percent of the amount of the payment which could be made without regard to this subsection, or

children.label: II
children.text: the present value (determined under guidance prescribed by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, using the interest and mortality assumptions under ) of the maximum guarantee with respect to the participant under .section 1055(g) of this titlesection 1322 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The plan shall also provide that only 1 prohibited payment meeting the requirements of clause (i) may be made with respect to any participant during any period of consecutive plan years to which the limitations under either subparagraph (A) or (B) or this subparagraph applies.

children.heading: Treatment of beneficiaries
children.label: II Treatment of beneficiaries
children.text: For purposes of this clause, a participant and any beneficiary on his behalf (including an alternate payee, as defined in subsection (d)(3)(K)) shall be treated as 1 participant. If the accrued benefit of a participant is allocated to such an alternate payee and 1 or more other persons, the amount under clause (i) shall be allocated among such persons in the same manner as the accrued benefit is allocated unless the qualified domestic relations order (as defined in subsection (d)(3)(B)(i)) provides otherwise.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: D Exception
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any plan for any plan year if the terms of such plan (as in effect for the period beginning on , and ending with such plan year) provide for no benefit accruals with respect to any participant during such period.September 1, 2005

children.label: i
children.text: any payment, in excess of the monthly amount paid under a single life annuity (plus any social security supplements described in the last sentence of ), to a participant or beneficiary whose annuity starting date (as defined in ) occurs during any period a limitation under subparagraph (A) or (B) is in effect,section 1054(b)(1)(G) of this titlesection 1055(h)(2) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: any payment for the purchase of an irrevocable commitment from an insurer to pay benefits, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any other payment specified by the Secretary of the Treasury by regulations.

children.heading: Limitation on benefit accruals for plans with severe funding shortfalls
children.label: 4 Limitation on benefit accruals for plans with severe funding shortfalls

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A defined benefit plan which is a single-employer plan shall provide that, in any case in which the plan’s adjusted funding target attainment percentage for a plan year is less than 60 percent, benefit accruals under the plan shall cease as of the valuation date for the plan year.

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: B Exemption
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall cease to apply with respect to any plan year, effective as of the first day of the plan year, upon payment by the plan sponsor of a contribution (in addition to any minimum required contribution under ) equal to the amount sufficient to result in an adjusted funding target attainment percentage of 60 percent.section 1083 of this title

children.heading: Rules relating to contributions required to avoid benefit limitations
children.label: 5 Rules relating to contributions required to avoid benefit limitations

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the adjusted funding target attainment percentage shall be determined by treating as an asset of the plan any security provided by a plan sponsor in a form meeting the requirements of clause (ii).

children.label: I
children.text: a bond issued by a corporate surety company that is an acceptable surety for purposes of ,section 1112 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: cash, or United States obligations which mature in 3 years or less, held in escrow by a bank or similar financial institution, or

children.label: III
children.text: such other form of security as is satisfactory to the Secretary of the Treasury and the parties involved.

children.label: I
children.text: the date on which the plan terminates,

children.label: II
children.text: if there is a failure to make a payment of the minimum required contribution for any plan year beginning after the security is provided, the due date for the payment under , orsection 1083(j) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: if the adjusted funding target attainment percentage is less than 60 percent for a consecutive period of 7 years, the valuation date for the last year in the period.

children.heading: Release of security
children.label: iv Release of security
children.text: The security shall be released (and any amounts thereunder shall be refunded together with any interest accrued thereon) at such time as the Secretary of the Treasury may prescribe in regulations, including regulations for partial releases of the security by reason of increases in the adjusted funding target attainment percentage.

children.heading: Prefunding balance or funding standard carryover balance may not be used
children.label: B Prefunding balance or funding standard carryover balance may not be used
children.text: No prefunding balance or funding standard carryover balance under may be used under paragraph (1), (2), or (4) to satisfy any payment an employer may make under any such paragraph to avoid or terminate the application of any limitation under such paragraph.section 1083(f) of this title

children.heading: Deemed reduction of funding balances
children.label: C Deemed reduction of funding balances

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (iii), in any case in which a benefit limitation under paragraph (1), (2), (3), or (4) would (but for this subparagraph and determined without regard to paragraph (1)(B), (2)(B), or (4)(B)) apply to such plan for the plan year, the plan sponsor of such plan shall be treated for purposes of this chapter as having made an election under to reduce the prefunding balance or funding standard carryover balance by such amount as is necessary for such benefit limitation to not apply to the plan for such plan year.section 1083(f) of this title

children.heading: Exception for insufficient funding balances
children.label: ii Exception for insufficient funding balances
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply with respect to a benefit limitation for any plan year if the application of clause (i) would not result in the benefit limitation not applying for such plan year.

children.heading: Restrictions of certain rules to collectively bargained plans
children.label: iii Restrictions of certain rules to collectively bargained plans
children.text: With respect to any benefit limitation under paragraph (1), (2), or (4), clause (i) shall only apply in the case of a plan maintained pursuant to 1 or more collective bargaining agreements between employee representatives and 1 or more employers.

children.heading: New plans
children.label: 6 New plans
children.text: Paragraphs (1), (2), and (4) shall not apply to a plan for the first 5 plan years of the plan. For purposes of this paragraph, the reference in this paragraph to a plan shall include a reference to any predecessor plan.

children.heading: Presumed underfunding for purposes of benefit limitations
children.label: 7 Presumed underfunding for purposes of benefit limitations

children.heading: Presumption of continued underfunding
children.label: A Presumption of continued underfunding
children.text: In any case in which a benefit limitation under paragraph (1), (2), (3), or (4) has been applied to a plan with respect to the plan year preceding the current plan year, the adjusted funding target attainment percentage of the plan for the current plan year shall be presumed to be equal to the adjusted funding target attainment percentage of the plan for the preceding plan year until the enrolled actuary of the plan certifies the actual adjusted funding target attainment percentage of the plan for the current plan year.

children.heading: Presumption of underfunding after 10th month
children.label: B Presumption of underfunding after 10th month
children.text: In any case in which no certification of the adjusted funding target attainment percentage for the current plan year is made with respect to the plan before the first day of the 10th month of such year, for purposes of paragraphs (1), (2), (3), and (4), such first day shall be deemed, for purposes of such paragraph, to be the valuation date of the plan for the current plan year and the plan’s adjusted funding target attainment percentage shall be conclusively presumed to be less than 60 percent as of such first day.

children.heading: Presumption of underfunding after 4th month for nearly underfunded plans
children.label: C Presumption of underfunding after 4th month for nearly underfunded plans
i
children.text: a benefit limitation under paragraph (1), (2), (3), or (4) did not apply to a plan with respect to the plan year preceding the current plan year, but the adjusted funding target attainment percentage of the plan for such preceding plan year was not more than 10 percentage points greater than the percentage which would have caused such paragraph to apply to the plan with respect to such preceding plan year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: as of the first day of the 4th month of the current plan year, the enrolled actuary of the plan has not certified the actual adjusted funding target attainment percentage of the plan for the current plan year,

children.heading: Treatment of plan as of close of prohibited or cessation period
children.label: 8 Treatment of plan as of close of prohibited or cessation period

children.heading: Operation of plan after period
children.label: A Operation of plan after period
children.text: Unless the plan provides otherwise, payments and accruals will resume effective as of the day following the close of the period for which any limitation of payment or accrual of benefits under paragraph (3) or (4) applies.

children.heading: Treatment of affected benefits
children.label: B Treatment of affected benefits
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed as affecting the plan’s treatment of benefits which would have been paid or accrued but for this subsection.

children.heading: Terms relating to funding target attainment percentage
children.label: 9 Terms relating to funding target attainment percentage

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “funding target attainment percentage” has the same meaning given such term by .section 1083(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Adjusted funding target attainment percentage
children.label: B Adjusted funding target attainment percentage
children.text: The term “adjusted funding target attainment percentage” means the funding target attainment percentage which is determined under subparagraph (A) by increasing each of the amounts under subparagraphs (A) and (B) of by the aggregate amount of purchases of annuities for employees other than highly compensated employees (as defined in ) which were made by the plan during the preceding 2 plan years.section 1083(d)(2) of this titlesection 414(q) of title 26

children.heading: Application to plans which are fully funded without regard to reductions for funding balances
children.label: C Application to plans which are fully funded without regard to reductions for funding balances
children.text: In the case of a plan for any plan year, if the funding target attainment percentage is 100 percent or more (determined without regard to the reduction in the value of assets under ), the funding target attainment percentage for purposes of subparagraphs (A) and (B) shall be determined without regard to such reduction.section 1083(f)(4) of this title

children.heading: Secretarial authority for plans with alternate valuation date
children.label: 10 Secretarial authority for plans with alternate valuation date
children.text: In the case of a plan which has designated a valuation date other than the first day of the plan year, the Secretary of the Treasury may prescribe rules for the application of this subsection which are necessary to reflect the alternate valuation date.

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 113–295, div. A, title II, § 221(a)(57)(G)(ii)128 Stat. 4047Dec. 19, 2014
children.label: 11 Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 113–295, div. A, title II, § 221(a)(57)(G)(ii)128 Stat. 4047Dec. 19, 2014

children.heading: CSEC plans
children.label: 12 CSEC plans
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to a CSEC plan (as defined in ).section 1060(f) of this title

children.heading: Special rules applicable to benefit overpayments
children.label: h Special rules applicable to benefit overpayments
A
children.text: any participant or beneficiary,

children.label: i
children.text: an individual account plan, provided that the amount needed to prevent or restore any impermissible forfeiture from any participant’s or beneficiary’s account arising in connection with the overpayment is, separately from and independently of the overpayment, allocated to such account pursuant to the nonforfeitability requirements of (for example, out of the plan’s forfeiture account, additional employer contributions, or recoveries from those responsible for the overpayment), orsection 1053 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a defined benefit pension plan subject to the funding rules in part 3 of this subtitle B, unless the responsible plan fiduciary determines, in the exercise of its fiduciary discretion, that failure to recover all or part of the overpayment faster than required under such funding rules would materially affect the plan’s ability to pay benefits due to other participants and beneficiaries, or

children.label: C
children.text: any fiduciary of the plan, other than a fiduciary (including a plan sponsor or contributing employer acting in a fiduciary capacity) whose breach of its fiduciary duties resulted in such overpayment, provided that if the plan has established prudent procedures to prevent and minimize overpayment of benefits and the relevant plan fiduciaries have followed such procedures, an inadvertent benefit overpayment will not give rise to a breach of fiduciary duty.

children.heading: Reduction in future benefit payments and recovery from responsible party
children.label: 2 Reduction in future benefit payments and recovery from responsible party
A
children.text: reduces future benefit payments to the correct amount provided for under the terms of the plan, or

children.label: B
children.text: seeks recovery from the person or persons responsible for the overpayment.

children.heading: Employer funding obligations
children.label: 3 Employer funding obligations
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall relieve an employer of any obligation imposed on it to make contributions to a plan to meet the minimum funding standards under part 3 of this subtitle B or to prevent or restore an impermissible forfeiture in accordance with .section 1053 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: No interest or other additional amounts (such as collection costs or fees) are sought on overpaid amounts for any period.

children.label: i
children.text: the reduction ceases after the plan has recovered the full dollar amount of the overpayment,

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount recouped each calendar year does not exceed 10 percent of the full dollar amount of the overpayment, and

children.label: iii
children.text: future benefit payments are not reduced to below 90 percent of the periodic amount otherwise payable under the terms of the plan.

children.label: C
children.text: If the plan seeks to recoup past overpayments of a benefit other than a non-decreasing annuity, the plan satisfies requirements developed by the Secretary of Labor for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.label: i
children.text: not accompanied by threats of litigation, unless the responsible plan fiduciary makes a determination that there is a reasonable likelihood of success to recover an amount greater than the cost of recovery, and

children.label: ii
children.text: not made through a collection agency or similar third party, unless the participant or beneficiary ignores or rejects efforts to recoup the overpayment following either a final judgment in Federal or State court or a settlement between the participant or beneficiary and the plan, in either case authorizing such recoupment.

children.label: E
children.text: Recoupment of past overpayments to a participant is not sought from any beneficiary of the participant, including a spouse, surviving spouse, former spouse, or other beneficiary.

children.label: F
children.text: Recoupment may not be sought if the first overpayment occurred more than 3 years before the participant or beneficiary is first notified in writing of the error, except in the case of fraud or misrepresentation by the participant.

children.label: i
children.text: such plan shall notify the plan receiving the rollover of such dispute,

children.label: ii
children.text: the plan receiving the rollover shall retain such overpayment on behalf of the participant or beneficiary (and shall be entitled to treat such overpayment as plan assets) pending the outcome of such procedures, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the portion of such overpayment with respect to which recoupment is sought on behalf of the plan shall be permitted to be returned to such plan if it is determined to be an overpayment (and the plans making and receiving such transfer shall be treated as permitting such transfer).

children.label: H
children.text: In determining the amount of recoupment to seek, the responsible plan fiduciary may take into account the hardship that recoupment likely would impose on the participant or beneficiary.

children.heading: Effect of culpability
children.label: 5 Effect of culpability
children.text: Subparagraphs (A) through (F) of paragraph (4) shall not apply to protect a participant or beneficiary who is culpable. For purposes of this paragraph, a participant or beneficiary is culpable if the individual bears responsibility for the overpayment (such as through misrepresentations or omissions that led to the overpayment), or if the individual knew that the benefit payment or payments were materially in excess of the correct amount. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, an individual is not culpable merely because the individual believed the benefit payment or payments were or might be in excess of the correct amount, if the individual raised that question with an authorized plan representative and was told the payment or payments were not in excess of the correct amount.
